role_title: Product Program Manager, Roadmap Planning & Program Management

location.office_time_required: must be based in San Francisco-Bay Area, CA

level_signals: 8+ years of experience in program management, product management, or business operations
Proven ability to build strong relationships with business leaders up to the executive level
Base pay range $176,000–$220,000 USD consistent with Senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of  experience in a combination of program management, product management, or business operations managing sophisticated software product programs from end to end in a creative-led, fast paced environment
Strong product and/or technology experience including product management or engineering (software preferred)
Demonstrated ability of shipping software products at global scale having overcome a variety of challenges
Critical thinking skills to develop bespoke program plans that are custom to the needs of the specific program
World-class verbal and written communication skills that can simplify complex topics
Proven ability to build strong relationships and be a trusted agent for change with business leaders (up to the executive level)
Track record for successfully managing ambitious and operationally complex projects with cross-functional teams across product development, product marketing, design, marketing, and operations
Expertise in using program management tools such as Airtable, Asana, Jira, Smartsheets, etc.
Thrives in ambiguity in a fast-paced, hyper-growth organization

scope.surface: Company roadmap planning and product program management; bi-annual releases
scope.business_model: consumer marketplace (B2C and B2B), short-term rental and experiences platform
primary_metrics_inferred: On-time and quality delivery of roadmap initiatives
Velocity and quality of cross-functional product programs
core_responsibilities: Partners with engineering, product marketing, design, community support, and other cross functional teams to deliver software product programs with the highest quality and on schedule
Understands dependencies across functions to optimize the overall system and deliver the highest quality offerings at scale
Anticipates upcoming risks and issues and develops risk mitigation strategies proactively in a fast pace environment
Brings an experienced product mindset to tie strategy and execution together
Develops program plans that are unique to each initiative and require strategic thinking
Operates at a variety of altitudes by tailoring communication style and approach
Goes beyond status reporting to drive issues to resolution
Understands best practices, drives improvements, and focuses on simplification

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as potential component alongside bonus and Employee Travel Credits

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed ($176,000–$220,000 USD base)
Clear team mission and role context provided (Roadmap Planning & Program Management)
Bonus, equity, and Employee Travel Credits mentioned as additional compensation components
Role sits at intersection of strategy and execution with high executive visibility
Inclusive hiring language and disability accommodation process explicitly described
red_flags: Must be physically based in San Francisco-Bay Area, CA — no remote or other location options for this role
Long list of excluded states (Alaska, Indiana, Nebraska, North Dakota, Ohio, South Dakota, Wisconsin, Alabama, Mississippi, Oklahoma, Delaware, Rhode Island) limits candidate pool significantly
No visa sponsorship status mentioned; unclear for international candidates
Excluded states list described as 'continuously being updated' — creates ongoing uncertainty for candidates in other states

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and years of experience requirement)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
location.visa_sponsorship (not mentioned in JD; marked unclear)
ambiguities: JD states the role requires being 'based in San Francisco-Bay Area, CA' but also references a list of excluded states rather than limiting purely to SF-Bay Area, suggesting some flexibility in location may exist for non-excluded states — treated as onsite/SF-required here.
Compensation note says 'job titles may span more than one career level' but no specific level is named — seniority inferred from comp range and experience requirement.
